Registry / Terms / calculator

calculator

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #00849 · 17 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 12 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

Ed.YearPublicationClauseDefinitionConsistency
20102008OIML R 85-1:2008
#02305
3.7
part of the ALG that receives the output signals from the transducer and, if applicable, from ancillary devices and/or other devices, processes them and, if appropriate, stores the results in memory until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with other devices
pending
20102007OIML R 117-1:2007
#01539
T.c.1
part of the meter that receives the output signals from the measuring device(s) and, possibly, from associated measuring devices, processes them and, if appropriate, stores the results in memory until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with ancillary devices
pending
20102007OIML R 140:2007
#02043
T.1.3
part of the measuring system that receives the output signals from the flow measuring device(s) or from another calculator and possibly from the associated measuring instruments, transforms them, and, if appropriate, stores the results in memory until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of transmitting and receiving data from peripheral equipment
pending
20102006OIML R 49-1:2006
#00414
2.1.4
part of the meter which receives the output signals from the transducer(s) and, possibly, from associated measuring instruments, transforms them and, if appropriate, stores the results in memory until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with ancillary devices
pending
20102006OIML R 137-1:2006
#01929
2.1.6
part of the gas meter which receives the output signals from the measuring transducer(s) and, possibly, associated measuring instruments, transforms them and, if appropriate, stores the results in memory until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with ancillary devices
pending
20102002OIML R 75-1:2002
#00849
3.4.3
a sub-assembly which receives signals from the flow sensor and the temperature sensors and calculates and indicates the quantity ofheat exchanged
pending
20101998OIML R 81:1998
#01016
3.7
a part of the meter that receives the output signal from the transducer(s), transforms it and, if appropriate, stores in memory the results until they are used. Additionally, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with the peripheral equipment
pending
20101993OIML R 105:1993
#01225
T.13.2
a device that receives the output quantity from the transducer(s), checks and transforms it and, if appropriate, memorizes the results until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with peripheral equipment
pending
202X2024OIML R049-1:2024
#02343
3.1.4
part of the meter that transforms the output signals from the measurement transducer(s) (3.1.2) and, possibly, from associated measuring instruments and stores the results in memory until they are used
pending
202X2022OIML R139-1:2022
#02915
3.2.4
association of metering calculator and operational calculator
pending
202X2017OIML R117-1:2017
#02914
T.c.1
part of the meter that receives the output signals from the measuring device(s) and, possiblyalso from associated measuring devices, processes them and, as required , stores the results in memory until they are used.
pending
202X2012OIML R137:2012
#02650
3.1.5
part of the gas meter which receives the output signals from the measuring transducer(s) and, possibly, associated measuring instruments, transforms them and, if appropriate, stores the results in memory until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with ancillary devices
pending
202X2008OIML R 85-1:2008

3.7
part of the ALG that receives the output signals from the transducer and, if applicable, from ancillary devices and/or other devices, processes them and, if appropriate, stores the results in memory until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with other devices
pending

#02043
T.1.3
part of the measuring system that receives the output signals from the flow measuring device(s) or from another calculator and possibly from the associated measuring instruments, transforms them, and, if appropriate, stores the results in memory until they are used. In addition, the calculator may be capable of transmitting and receiving data from peripheral equipment
pending

a sub-assembly which receives signals from the flow sensor and the temperature sensors and calculates and indicates the quantity of heat exchanged

#01016
3.7
a part of the meter that receives the output signal from the transducer(s), transforms it and, if appropriate, stores in memory the results until they are used. Additionally, the calculator may be capable of communicating both ways with the peripheral equipment
